item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
• If there are weird or unusal spaces, explicitly state what aspects of the space defy physical laws or don't.
… If a substance is unidentified, it should be analyzed and the results should be given because that's the point of a report. I

I suspect you're conflating what an SCP article is (a piece of flash-fiction) for what it simulates (a scientific report). Those familiar with reports of this nature might notice these oversights, but — much as the target audience for Iron Man isn't an actual engineer — the target audience for SCP articles aren't actual scientists (though I'm sure some scientists enjoy reading them!). The goal is to create a simulacra convincing enough to fool a layman… not reproduce a 'genuine' report.

There's wiggle room here, obviously; different articles extend more (or less) deeply into 'authenticity'. But I strongly disagree that the elements you're pointing out here represent fundamental errors. Missed opportunities, maybe — but I don't think most people reading this would take issue that a substance is simply described as 'unidentified'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If you're going to provide no punchline besides "this is weird idk man," the following would make the story pop:

• A good reason why the pheonomenon was discovered in the first place, with details clearly indicating that the weird stuff/bad things were a component of the thing being studied, giving the reader a clear understanding as to how investigation began in the first place.

• If there are weird or unusal spaces, explicitly state what aspects of the space defy physical laws or don't.

I can't stress that second one enough. Rooms don't have to be on blueprints. If a house has a ton of un-enterable rooms, you'll notice the unused space if you live there — or even if you just visit it. If the house defies the laws of physics, that's something you need to state up-front, then describe the investigation into the same. If pipes do not have a clear function, there are completely mundane ways of tracking their layout and such should be described. If a substance is unidentified, it should be analyzed and the results should be given because that's the point of a report. It's hard to be made unsettled by a demon house when the incomplete report of its researchers seems far more absurd.

Downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How did they pinpoint that the house was the center of the anomaly? I like the idea of really not telling the reader much other than why it’s weird and a bunch of disappearances have occurred in the area… but I find myself pulled out by the fact that I have no idea why the foundation was drawn to it in the first place. High number of disappearances aside, why look at this house specifically?

item.title: Novote.... for now
item.content:encoded:
I really want to upvote this SCP… It’s very well written, every single word makes its own contribution to the overall narrative, which made this SCP really difficult to put down when I started reading. I also like the use of images- they are really well incorporated into the exploration logs, as opposed to having simply been pasted into a pre-existing block of text (which I believe to be the cardinal sin of image use).

However… when I reached the end, my first thought was ‘is that it?’. This SCP gives very little information about the anomaly to the reader. In fact, there is so little information here that it can even be debated whether this SCP is actually anomalous or not- while extremely unlikely, many people disappearing within a small area could have a perfectly mundane explanation, as could all the other weird things mentioned.

I get that leaving the reader in the dark about certain things can have a positive effect, but there is so much information missing here that it’s difficult to work out what this even is. That may appeal to some readers, but unfortunately doesn’t work for me.

Novote, but I constantly find myself coming back to read this, so I may change my verdict in the future.

item.title: SCP-5344: +1
item.content:encoded:
Hesitant upvote. This was definitely a good spooky article without a redaction in sight, and I loved how thorough the investigation felt, but I think you could do just a little more to ramp up the weirdness of the house in compensation for the boring ending.

EDIT: I do not think this SCP or any part of it is boring. I meant that the ending is relatively actionless/plotless compared to the rest. It's a decent ending, just really un-interesting the current way the whole SCP is written.

item.title: hesitant +1
item.content:encoded:
I wanna like this but… eh. I get the idea of wanting to build escalation up to nothing, but im kind of lost on that "what the fuck?" feeling, more just doesnt make sense. The anomalies are strange but it does feel very parawatchy as in like… this could be real maybe? It's a realistic anomalous. It's wierd. And the more I type about this the more I start to like it to the point of wanting to novote at least, but I'm not going to edit the rest of the stuff i just typed, because it shows my thought process.

And here I am, about to submit, and I just reread it again, and I think I like it a lot? So I'm giving a hesitant upvote. The images really help, because its just pure spooky, and I love that vibe from images.

In all, this SCP is really conflicting with my standards, and it's hard to get some solid thoughts on it for me. But I think I like it? I'm not sure. My vote might change later.

edit: upon some rethinking and another reread, I've swapped to a novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is one of those articles that's a really hard sell… but that's actually kind of why I like it (and why I wanted to try it).

I regret being too busy to really jump back into working on this with you, but I'm still quite fond of how it came out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I fuck with it. A pure concept taking all the hallmarks of skips with something going on, but purposefully not trying to form something at the end. I can see how some people might not enjoy being blueballes by the anti-story, but it's good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While I can kind of see what you're getting at, I didn't find it particularly interesting.

item.title: One author's note
item.content:encoded:
This is something The Great Hippo and I worked on at various times in various ways. Then it sat around for a while and I fiddled with it some more. It's probably about something. We can only assume, at least.

Image sources below. Modified by Hippo where applicable.

House image

Room image

Crawlspace image

Good night.
